description: OpenAI function calling schemas for Verse Protocol — use with Codex, GPT agents, or any OpenAI-compatible agent

functions.name: verse_status
functions.description: View the current verse DAG — all narrative objects, their types, relations, and connections. Call this first to understand the world before making changes.

functions.name: verse_extend
functions.description: Create a new narrative object that extends an existing node. This adds structure without contradicting the parent. Use for adding characters to locations, systems to worlds, events to timelines, etc.

parentId.description: ID of the parent node to extend (e.g., 'v1'). Run verse_status first to see available nodes.

name.description: Name of the new narrative object (e.g., 'The Ember Vaults')

type.description: Type of narrative object to create

description.description: Short description (1-3 sentences) of the narrative object

content.description: Full lore/prose in markdown format. Include world-building details, rules, character backstory, etc. The richer the content, the higher the influence score.

functions.name: verse_fork
functions.description: Create a divergent fork from an existing node. This creates a branch where you modify or contradict the parent's canon. Use for 'what if' scenarios, alternate timelines, or reinterpretations.

parentId.description: ID of the node to fork from

description.description: How this diverges from the parent

functions.name: verse_merge
functions.description: Synthesize multiple narrative threads into a new unified verse. This is the highest-creativity operation — it creates connective tissue between independent narratives. High merge centrality is strongly rewarded in influence scoring.

description.description: How these threads combine and what new meaning emerges

functions.name: verse_read_lore
functions.description: Read the full lore/prose content for a specific verse node. Use this to understand a node's world-building before extending, forking, or merging it.

functions.name: verse_score
functions.description: Compute and display influence scores for all nodes. Shows fork depth, structural reuse, merge centrality, novelty delta, and total score.

functions.name: verse_affordances
functions.description: Detect expansion opportunities — gaps in the world that invite new content. Returns unexplored regions, character vacancies, open plotlines, and undefined systems.

functions.name: verse_search
functions.description: Search verse nodes by label, type, author, or description text.

query.description: Search text to match against node fields
